summary: Fixed a Windows build quoting issue in configure.py by changing the DolphinLib compiler flag for `-pragma cats off` from a single-quoted shell-style argument to a double-quoted form inside a Python single-quoted string. The PR body states that single quotes do not work on Windows.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; adjusts the DolphinLib compiler argument list to avoid Windows-incompatible single quotes.

what_changed.change: Replaced `"-pragma 'cats off'"` with `'-pragma "cats off"'` in the DolphinLib configuration flags.
what_changed.evidence: Diff hunk in DolphinLib shows exactly one line changed.

what_changed.change: Kept the semantic compiler pragma argument `cats off` unchanged while altering only the quoting style used to pass it.
what_changed.evidence: Before and after lines both pass `-pragma` with `cats off`; only single vs double quotes differ.

smart_moves: Minimized the Windows fix to a one-line quoting change, avoiding broader build-system churn.
Preserved the actual DolphinLib pragma text while changing only the command argument quoting, reducing risk to non-Windows behavior.
Used Python string quoting to emit double quotes around `cats off`, which is more portable for Windows command invocation than shell single quotes.
decomp_lessons: Build flags that rely on Unix shell single-quote behavior can fail on Windows; prefer quoting forms that survive Windows command parsing.
For multi-word compiler or tool arguments in configure.py, verify how the generated command line is interpreted on all supported host platforms.
Small host-toolchain fixes can be important for keeping decompilation workflows reproducible across contributors' systems.
follow_up_queries: Find other configure.py tool flags that use embedded single quotes and may break on Windows.
Check whether DolphinLib or other build helpers pass multi-word arguments through shell parsing on Windows.
Search for `-pragma` arguments in the build configuration to ensure consistent portable quoting.
